author: taratorio
content: a summary of my findings : https://gist.github.com/taratorio/bbaab9c6a816b8488277a04cef316c40

author: taratorio
content: The 6th node in the bootnode list seems to be a generic network crawler which apparently has both CL and EL peers (?) and predominantly gives us back CL peers with which we correctly fail to connect with. The crawler seems to have 5x more CL peers than EL peers, so the saturation of its neightbours is CL heavy.

author: taratorio
content: that bit about the network crawler is a bit sus - where is the repo for it? i can quickly check

author: taratorio
content: <@277500722655002624> also would you be able to check Nethermind nodes to verify the discv4 vs discv5 please?

author: taratorio
content: <@412614104222531604> if you update bootnodes set B to match set A from my summary that should unblock us since we will be able to connect to disv5 compatible ethrex nodes

author: edgl_dev
content: can confirm ethrex runs both discv4 and discv5

author: barnabasbusa
content: i’ll check in a bit

author: barnabasbusa
content: but nethermind not supporting v5 seems like a bug?

author: taratorio
content:
yes, as long as the findings are correct, waiting on <@277500722655002624> to confirm

there is also the question about whether we should be running disv4 & discv5 at the same time. my thoughts are that if the target is to drop discv4 entirely by glamsterdam, we should explicitly only run with discv5 on devnets as it's a good opportunity to move all clients at the same time

author: barnabasbusa
content: I agree most clients should default to v5

author: taratorio
content:
btw might be helpful if you enable `--log.console.verbosity=debug` for those 2 nodes (prysm and teku ones) because the only logging that feeds to clickhouse is info and that barely has any useful info for this type of issue.

tbh might even be good to do `trace` temporarily until we figure out what is going on

author: __flcl
content: <@199561711278227457>  `--Discovery.UseDefaultDiscv5Bootnodes=false` can be added to exclude initial participation in the common discv5 network

author: barnabasbusa
content: why would you wanna disable this?

author: barnabasbusa
content: we should be defaulting to v5

author: barnabasbusa

author: __flcl
content: UseDefaultDiscv5Bootnodes does not disable discv5, it just additionally adds this to bootnodes https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/blob/master/src/Nethermind/Nethermind.Network.Discovery/Discv5/discv5-bootnodes.json
